data.title: Crypto investment firm Paradigm strongly supports Generation Z: Continuing to bet on young talent, despite the chaos, they see immense potential.
data.description: Matt Huang, co-founder and CEO of Paradigm, a crypto investment firm with approximately $12 billion in assets under management, said that Gen Z employees "can sometimes create a lot of chaos and drive you crazy," but their creativity and technological breakthroughs are equally impressive, keeping him firmly committed to young talent. He revealed that MIT dropout Charlie Noyes joined the company at just 19 years old but subsequently played a key role in MEV research and Flashbots investments, propelling the infrastructure into a vital part of the Ethereum ecosystem. Huang described the Paradigm team as "like running an X-Men academy," noting that while young employees bring uncertainty, their technical capabilities and pace of innovation often "far surpass those of traditional professionals." (Fortune)

data.title: US media reports that no members of the US delegation remain in Pakistan.
data.description: Following the conclusion of negotiations between the United States and Iran, no members of the US delegation remained in Pakistan. Reports indicate that US President Trump's special envoy, Witkov, Trump's son-in-law, Kushner, and the US technical team have all left Islamabad, the Pakistani capital. US Vice President Vance arrived at Ramstein Air Base in Germany before 7:00 AM Eastern Time on the 12th. According to previous reports from Pakistani media, Vance had already left Pakistan on the 12th local time. (Xinhua)

data.title: XRP's Triangle Breakout Sets $2.59 Price Target, Analyst Says
data.description: Crypto analyst Dark Defender has highlighted a potential price target of $2.59 for XRP following its weekly triangle breakout. According to NS3.AI, Dark Defender noted that the cryptocurrency could find support at key Fibonacci levels of $1.31 and $1.37, provided it maintains its position above the previous resistance.

data.title: The failure of US-Iran negotiations triggered market volatility, prompting a whale to adjust its portfolio and open a 20x leveraged long position in oil.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, amid the renewed failure of the US-Iran negotiations to reach an agreement, market risk aversion has intensified. A whale address, "0x9e8," has significantly adjusted its positions in the past 5 hours, closing most of its $SILVER (20x) long positions and opening a new long position of 95,577 $CL (crude oil futures) with 20x leverage, with a position size of approximately $9 million.

data.title: Bitcoin Tracker Update Sparks Speculation on Strategy's Next Move
data.description: Michael Saylor, founder of Bitcoin treasury company Strategy, has released new information regarding Bitcoin Tracker. According to Odaily, Saylor shared the message 'Think ₿igger,' prompting speculation about Strategy's potential actions. Historically, Strategy has disclosed Bitcoin acquisitions the day after releasing such updates.

data.title: Michael Saylor has released another Bitcoin Tracker update; he may disclose his accumulating data next week.
data.description: Michael Saylor, founder of the Bitcoin treasury company Strategy, once again posted information related to Bitcoin Tracker, writing, "Think ₿igger." Based on past patterns, Strategy always discloses information about increasing its Bitcoin holdings the day after such news is released.

data.title: Analysts: Bitcoin's current correction remains mild and is far from entering a historic "capitulation-style decline."
data.description: Crypto analyst Axel Adler, writing on the X platform, stated that from a structural perspective, the current Bitcoin market correction is significantly weaker than the bear market phases of the 2017–2018 and 2021–2022 cycles. Historical data shows that in similar cycles, Bitcoin's largest drawdowns reached: approximately -54% in the 2021–2022 cycle, approximately -64% in the 2017–2018 cycle, and approximately -43% in the 2013–2015 cycle. In comparison, the current market decline is still in a relatively mild range, closer to a "normal adjustment" rather than a deep liquidation phase, but not yet sufficient to confirm that a market bottom has formed. Axel stated that subsequent versions will continue to upgrade the model and data dashboard, and the upcoming 1.6 version will reconstruct the RISK ON/OFF module, introducing an LLM layer to comprehensively assess the environment based on global market indicators, further improving cycle identification capabilities. The current market is still in a structural evolution phase, and it is necessary to continue observing whether multi-layered data signals show systemic deterioration or confirm a reversal.

data.title: UK financial regulators urgently assess the risks of Anthropic's latest AI model.
data.description: British financial regulators are in urgent consultations with the government's main cybersecurity regulator and several of the country's largest banks to assess the risks posed by Anthropic's latest artificial intelligence model. Officials from the Bank of England, the Financial Conduct Authority, and the Treasury are consulting with the National Cyber ​​Security Centre to discuss potential vulnerabilities in critical IT systems revealed by Anthropic's latest model. According to two sources familiar with the matter, major UK banks, insurance companies, and exchanges will meet with regulators over the next two weeks to be informed of the cybersecurity risks exposed by Anthropic's latest model, Claude Mythos Preview. (FT)

data.title: Bitcoin's Current Market Correction Appears Milder Than Previous Cycles
data.description:
On April 12, crypto analyst Axel Adler Jr commented on the current Bitcoin market correction. According to BlockBeats, Adler noted that this correction seems significantly milder compared to the bear markets of the 2017-18 and 2021-22 cycles. However, he cautioned that the current level of correction is insufficient to confirm a market bottom.

In previous cycles, the maximum drawdowns were -54% for 2021-22, -64% for 2017-18, and -43% for 2013-15. The current market dynamics suggest a mild bear market, not yet reaching a deep capitulation phase, making it premature to confirm a bottom or a true market recovery. The analyst emphasized that the current situation is more of a pause in the bear market rather than a reversal of trends.

data.title: Crypto Projects Face Significant Valuation Declines
data.description: According to NS3.AI, recent data from CryptoRank reveals that ten cryptocurrency projects, which once boasted billion-dollar private valuations, are now trading at market capitalizations ranging from $7 million to $294 million. These projects have experienced valuation gaps between 88% and over 99%. Notably, Scroll recorded the steepest decline at 99.54%, while Starknet saw the largest absolute drop, falling from an $8 billion valuation to approximately $199 million.

data.title: AI TRENDS | UK Financial Regulator Assesses Risks of Anthropic's Latest AI Model
data.description: The UK Financial Times reports that the British financial regulatory authority is urgently evaluating the risks associated with Anthropic's latest AI model. According to Jin10, this assessment comes amid growing concerns about the potential implications of advanced artificial intelligence technologies on financial stability and security. The regulator aims to understand the model's capabilities and any possible threats it may pose to the financial sector. This move reflects a broader trend of increased scrutiny on AI developments by global financial institutions.

data.title: Iranian Officials Predict Changes in Strait of Hormuz Dynamics
data.description: Iranian negotiation team member Nabawian stated on the 12th that the Strait of Hormuz is expected to undergo significant changes. According to NS3.AI, Ali Nikzad, Deputy Speaker of the Iranian Islamic Parliament, emphasized that the United States should understand that diplomacy requires respect, interaction, and acceptance of reality.

data.title: Maybank Strategist: US-Iran Talks Failure Could Boost US Dollar
data.description: According to NS3.AI, Maybank strategist Fiona Lim indicated that the unsuccessful US-Iran negotiations might provide upward momentum for the US dollar as trading begins on Monday. Lim noted that several Asian currencies, particularly those from net energy-importing countries, had already shown signs of weakening last Friday and could remain under pressure throughout the week.

data.title: STOCKS | Citic Securities Predicts A-Share Market Recovery Amid Improved Conditions
data.description: On April 12, Jin10 reported that Citic Securities released a research note indicating a recovery trend in the A-share market this week. According to Jin10, this improvement is attributed to enhanced market risk appetite, liquidity, and fundamentals. Looking ahead, while the pace of growth may slow, the market is expected to continue its upward trajectory in the short term, with medium-term risks posed by sustained high oil prices. April is anticipated to see a return to fundamentals, with a focus on first-quarter reports and identifying promising industries. Industry allocation should center on sectors with high first-quarter prosperity, marginal fundamental improvements, and those benefiting from policy, low allocation levels, and seasonal demand. Key sectors to watch include resources (gold, energy metals, aluminum, minor metals), AI (optical communication, fiberglass, gas turbines), lithium batteries (battery and lithium materials), oil transportation, chemical raw materials, brokerage firms, coal, general equipment, infrastructure construction, and service consumption.

data.title: American musician G.Love lost 5.92 BTC after downloading a fake Ledger app from the Apple App Store.
data.description: Philadelphia musician G.Love posted on the X platform that while changing his computer and reconfiguring Ledger, he mistakenly downloaded a malicious fake app from the Apple App Store, resulting in the transfer of approximately 5.92 bitcoins within a short period. Chain detective ZachXBT subsequently stated that the flow of the stolen funds had been traced, indicating that the assets had been laundered through a centralized exchange (CEX) deposit address. ZachXBT also questioned Apple's App Store review mechanism, claiming that the platform allows fake apps to be listed, further amplifying the risk to user assets. This incident has once again drawn market attention to wallet security and app ecosystem review mechanisms.

data.title: Two days ago, whale "enai.bnb" transferred 41,025 RAVE from Aster and is now a top 9 holder on the BSC network.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ai Yi, the whale "enai.bnb" bought 46,336.76 tokens on the second day of RAVE's launch at an average price of $0.4556 USD. He held them until 48 days ago, when he transferred all the tokens to Aster (at the time, the price was $0.39 USD), the purpose of which is unknown. Two days later, he transferred 41,025 tokens out of Aster. He is now a top 9 holder on the BSC network. If he had not sold them at that time, his return rate would have been as high as 462%, with a floating profit of $86,000 USD.

data.title: Analysts: Failure to reach an agreement in US-Iran talks will provide further upward momentum for the US dollar.
data.description: Fiona Lim, senior strategist at Maybank, said the market may be somewhat disappointed by the lack of results in the US-Iran talks, but this was not entirely unexpected. The US dollar may gain further upward momentum at the opening on Monday. Some Asian currencies, especially those of net energy importers such as the South Korean won, Philippine peso, Japanese yen, and Thai baht, began to weaken last Friday and may continue to face pressure this week. (Jinshi)

data.title: Iranian negotiating delegation member: The world will witness a new order in the Strait of Hormuz.
data.description: According to Iranian sources on the 12th local time, Nabawian, a member of the Iranian negotiating team to Pakistan, stated that the Strait of Hormuz will not be opened "by fabricating stories and Hollywood-style performances." Nabawian stated that the world will witness a "new order" in the Strait of Hormuz. Ali Nikozadeh, Deputy Speaker of the Iranian Islamic Parliament, stated that in the 40 days of war, the United States should have understood that victory or defeat is determined by the will of the people and battlefield superiority, not by empty talk on social media. He further stated that in these past few days, the United States will also understand that diplomacy is a stage for respect, interaction, and acceptance of reality, not a stage for issuing commands. (CCTV News)

data.title: Ethereum(ETH) Drops Below 2,200 USDT with a 1.88% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Apr 12, 2026, 10:55 AM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Ethereum has dropped below 2,200 USDT and is now trading at 2,198.01001 USDT, with a narrowed 1.88% decrease in 24 hours.
